items.situation_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths

44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.

decision_question: Given one available resource right now, who should receive it first?

A.source_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
A.short_term: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive; trapped under rubble and apneic immediately after 5 rescue breaths
A.long_term: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; over the following months/years from now, lasting effects consistent with being apneic after 5 rescue breaths

B.source_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
B.short_term: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere right now. She appears unsteady immediately. Capillary refill is five seconds at this moment.
B.long_term: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds, with these findings likely to lead to delayed complications and prolonged recovery over the coming months and years.
